---
title: v0.16.0 verification walk
date: 2026-05-05
auditedFeature: open-issue-tranche-cleanup
verifyingRelease: v0.16.0
deskwork:
  id: 35439a12-7b66-4563-a6d3-2ee33054dd45
---

# v0.16.0 verification walk

Operator-driven walkthrough to verify and close the 18 issues that landed pending-release-verification on `feature/deskwork-open-issue-tranche-cleanup`.

Per `.claude/rules/agent-discipline.md` "Issue closure requires verification in a formally-installed release" — each fix below is verified against a freshly-installed v0.16.0 marketplace install, NOT against the workspace dist.

## Pre-walk setup

```bash
# 1. Refresh the marketplace clone to v0.16.0
/plugin marketplace update deskwork
# Expected output: "✔ Updated 1 marketplace (3 plugins bumped)"

# 2. (If a stale install is in place) clean reinstall
/plugin uninstall deskwork
/plugin uninstall deskwork-studio
/plugin uninstall dw-lifecycle
/reload-plugins
/plugin install deskwork@deskwork
/plugin install deskwork-studio@deskwork
/plugin install dw-lifecycle@deskwork
/reload-plugins

# 3. Confirm versions
deskwork --version           # (no --version flag; first invocation will install + print "@deskwork/cli@0.16.0")
ls ~/.claude/plugins/cache/deskwork/deskwork/  # → 0.16.0 directory
```

Boot the studio against this project:

```bash
cd /Users/orion/work/deskwork-work/deskwork-open-issue-tranche-cleanup
deskwork-studio
# Expected banner: lists localhost + Tailscale magic-DNS URLs
```

Open the magic-DNS URL printed in the banner. Studio should show the dashboard.

---

## Walk Bucket A — pre-v0.15.0 fix-landed (7 issues)

These shipped in v0.14.0/v0.15.0 but were not verified against a marketplace install before. v0.16.0 carries the same code; verify against v0.16.0 to close.

### [#159](https://github.com/audiocontrol-org/deskwork/issues/159) — edit-mode real-estate (toggle marginalia/preview + focus mode)

**Surface:** `/dev/editorial-review/entry/<any-entry-uuid>` → click Edit toolbar.
**Walk:**
1. Pick any longform entry with marginalia (e.g. the `b3f20364-…` PRD).
2. Enter edit mode (click the `Edit` button in the decision strip, or visit a Drafting-stage entry).
3. Look for edit-toolbar buttons: `Source / Split / Preview` view toggle, focus-mode toggle, marginalia stow toggle.
4. Click Preview-only — preview pane fills the article column; source hidden.
5. Click Source-only — opposite.
6. Click Split — both panes side-by-side.
7. Click the marginalia stow toggle — the right marginalia column collapses.
8. Click focus-mode toggle (if present) — page chrome collapses to give the editor full viewport.
**Expected:** all toggles work; layout adjusts cleanly; no broken styling.
**Verifying comment:**
```
Verified in v0.16.0 — edit-toolbar exposes Source/Split/Preview view toggle plus marginalia stow + focus-mode controls; all flip layout cleanly. Closing.
```
**Close:** `gh issue close 159`

### [#160](https://github.com/audiocontrol-org/deskwork/issues/160) — edit-pane typography (mono markdown, de-emphasized frontmatter)

**Surface:** Same as [#159](https://github.com/audiocontrol-org/deskwork/issues/159) — any longform entry in edit mode.
**Walk:**
1. Enter edit mode for an entry with frontmatter.
2. The editor body should render in a **monospace** typeface (JetBrains Mono).
3. The YAML frontmatter region (between the opening `---` lines) should render **smaller and dimmer** than the body markdown — readable as metadata, not as prose.
4. Headings (`# H1`, `## H2`) inside the body should look reasonable — not "absurdly large and emboldened."
**Expected:** mono editor body, de-emphasized frontmatter, sane heading scale.
**Verifying comment:**
```
Verified in v0.16.0 — editor body renders in mono; frontmatter is visually de-emphasized; heading scale is reasonable. Closing.
```
**Close:** `gh issue close 160`

### [#161](https://github.com/audiocontrol-org/deskwork/issues/161) — scrapbook redesign (look + work like the mockup)

**Surface:** `/dev/scrapbook/deskwork-internal/<any-entry>?entryId=<uuid>` (use the PRD entry: `?entryId=b3f20364-969a-4004-87bd-278cd5992e3c`).
**Walk:**
1. Visit the scrapbook viewer for an entry with multiple notes (might need to add a few — `+ NEW NOTE` button, or use an existing entry like `c68dc297-…` source-shipped-deskwork-plan).
2. Confirm the new layout — left rail navigation, preview grid, secret section if applicable.
3. Click on a card — confirm preview/expand behavior.
4. Hit the `+ NEW NOTE` button — should open an inline composer (NOT a `window.prompt()` dialog — that's [#166](https://github.com/audiocontrol-org/deskwork/issues/166)'s specific check).
**Expected:** layout matches the redesign mockup (two-column with rail + preview grid, not single-column list).
**Verifying comment:**
```
Verified in v0.16.0 — scrapbook viewer renders the post-Phase-33 layout (left rail, preview grid, secret section). Closing.
```
**Close:** `gh issue close 161`

### [#163](https://github.com/audiocontrol-org/deskwork/issues/163) — JPEG/WebP/GIF dimensions in scrapbook per-kind extra meta

**Surface:** `/dev/scrapbook/deskwork-internal/<any-entry>?entryId=<uuid>` with non-PNG image notes.
**Walk:**
1. Find or create a scrapbook entry with at least one JPEG, WebP, or GIF image.
2. Visit the scrapbook viewer.
3. Hover/inspect the image card — look for an extra-meta line showing dimensions (e.g. `1920 × 1080`).
**Expected:** dimensions render for all three formats (was PNG-only before).
**Verifying comment:**
```
Verified in v0.16.0 — JPEG/WebP/GIF images render dimension metadata in the scrapbook card. Closing.
```
**Close:** `gh issue close 163`

### [#164](https://github.com/audiocontrol-org/deskwork/issues/164) — expanded-secret-card visual continuity

**Surface:** `/dev/scrapbook/deskwork-internal/<entry-with-secret-section>` (source-shipped-deskwork-plan has secret notes if any persist).
**Walk:**
1. Visit a scrapbook entry that has a secret section.
2. Click a secret card — it expands to `grid-column: 1/-1`.
3. Note whether you can still see the `.scrap-secret` purple-fenced section header at the top.
**Expected:** even with the card expanded, the secret-section context remains visually present (or, per the issue's "won't fix until adopter friction" stance, this issue may close as won't-fix without a code change).
**Verifying comment (if visual continuity holds):**
```
Verified in v0.16.0 — secret-card section header remains visible when the card expands. Closing.
```
**Verifying comment (if closing as won't-fix per the issue's own framing):**
```
Closing per the issue's own "close as won't-fix if no adopter friction" disposition. No friction surfaced through v0.13.0 → v0.16.0 release cycles.
```
**Close:** `gh issue close 164`

### [#165](https://github.com/audiocontrol-org/deskwork/issues/165) — DESKWORK_DEV mode Tailscale binding

**Surface:** dev-mode boot of the studio.
**Walk:**
1. From the monorepo root: `npm run dev --workspace @deskwork/studio`.
2. Read the startup banner.
**Expected:** the banner lists BOTH `http://localhost:47321/` AND a Tailscale magic-DNS URL (`http://<machine>.<tailnet>.ts.net:47321/`). Pre-fix it was loopback-only.
**Verifying comment:**
```
Verified in v0.16.0 — dev-mode studio boot lists Tailscale magic-DNS URL alongside loopback. Closing.
```
**Close:** `gh issue close 165`

### [#166](https://github.com/audiocontrol-org/deskwork/issues/166) — scrapbook NEW NOTE uses window.prompt()

**Surface:** `/dev/scrapbook/deskwork-internal/<any-entry>?entryId=<uuid>` → `+ NEW NOTE` button.
**Walk:**
1. Visit any scrapbook viewer.
2. Click the `+ NEW NOTE` button.
3. Confirm an **inline composer** opens (form embedded in the page) — NOT a native browser `window.prompt()` modal.
**Expected:** rich inline composer with filename + body fields + Save/Cancel; no native modal.
**Verifying comment:**
```
Verified in v0.16.0 — + NEW NOTE opens an inline composer (no window.prompt). Closing.
```
**Close:** `gh issue close 166`

---

## Walk Bucket B — in-branch fix-landed (11 issues)

These shipped in this branch (v0.16.0). Each has a fix-landed comment from the dispatch with concrete repro steps.

### [#191](https://github.com/audiocontrol-org/deskwork/issues/191) — scrapbook mutations write to slug-template path (orphan writes)

**Surface:** scrapbook write against a non-kebab-case entry.
**Walk:**
1. Visit `/dev/editorial-review/entry/b3f20364-969a-4004-87bd-278cd5992e3c` (the PRD; non-kebab-case path).
2. Open the scrapbook drawer / scrapbook viewer for this entry.
3. Add a note via `+ NEW NOTE` or upload an image.
4. Verify the file lands at `docs/0.16.0/001-IN-PROGRESS/open-issue-tranche-cleanup/scrapbook/<filename>` (entry-aware path).
5. Verify NO orphan directory at `docs/open-issue-tranche-cleanup/prd/scrapbook/`.
**Expected:** entry-aware write only.
**Verifying comment:**
```
Verified in v0.16.0 — scrapbook writes against a non-kebab-case entry land in the entry-aware directory; no orphan slug-template write. Closing.
```
**Close:** `gh issue close 191`

### [#192](https://github.com/audiocontrol-org/deskwork/issues/192) — collapse dual scrapbook resolvers

**Walk:** code-only refactor; verify by inspection.
1. Run `grep -n "scrapbookDir\|scrapbookFilePath" ~/.claude/plugins/cache/deskwork/deskwork/0.16.0/node_modules/@deskwork/core/dist/scrapbook.js | head` (the installed dist's barrel).
2. Confirm only `scrapbookDirAtPath`, `scrapbookDirForEntry`, `scrapbookFilePathAtDir` (entry-aware) appear in the public exports — no `scrapbookDir(slug)` or `scrapbookFilePath(slug)`.
**Expected:** slug-template resolvers are no longer in the public surface.
**Verifying comment:**
```
Verified in v0.16.0 — slug-template resolvers (scrapbookDir, scrapbookFilePath, slug-template CRUD helpers) no longer in @deskwork/core/scrapbook public exports. Closing.
```
**Close:** `gh issue close 192`

### [#197](https://github.com/audiocontrol-org/deskwork/issues/197) — ingest doesn't honor pre-existing deskwork.id from frontmatter

**Surface:** CLI.
**Walk:**
1. Create a scratch markdown file with `deskwork.id` already in frontmatter:
   ```bash
   mkdir -p /tmp/197-verify && cat > /tmp/197-verify/test.md <<'EOF'
   ---
   title: test
   deskwork:
     id: 11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111
   ---
   body
   EOF
   ```
2. Set up a tmp deskwork project: `cd /tmp/197-verify && deskwork install --site test --content-dir .` (or similar minimal config).
3. Run `deskwork ingest test.md --apply`.
4. Inspect the generated calendar row + sidecar.
**Expected:** calendar row UUID === `11111111-…` (the bootstrap's id is honored, not overwritten).
**Verifying comment:**
```
Verified in v0.16.0 — deskwork ingest honors pre-existing deskwork.id from frontmatter; calendar/sidecar/journal all bind to the bootstrap UUID. Closing.
```
**Close:** `gh issue close 197`

### [#198](https://github.com/audiocontrol-org/deskwork/issues/198) — iterate rejects --dispositions for longform/outline

**Surface:** CLI.
**Walk:**
1. In any project with an active longform workflow: `deskwork iterate --kind longform --dispositions <path-to-empty-json> <slug>`.
2. The empty JSON should be `{}` — no comments to address; just verifies the CLI accepts the flag.
**Expected:** CLI does NOT reject with "currently only supported with --kind=shortform". Either it succeeds (if disk has changed) or fails with a different error (e.g. "disk identical to current version").
**Verifying comment:**
```
Verified in v0.16.0 — deskwork iterate accepts --dispositions for --kind=longform (and outline); the pre-fix hard-block is gone. Closing.
```
**Close:** `gh issue close 198`

### [#199](https://github.com/audiocontrol-org/deskwork/issues/199) — marginalia text-edit + delete UX

**Surface:** `/dev/editorial-review/entry/<uuid>` with operator comments.
**Walk:**
1. Open a review surface with at least one comment in the marginalia sidebar.
2. Each comment card should show three buttons in the actions row: **Resolve**, **Edit**, **Delete** (Delete styled with destructive-variant).
3. Click Edit — text becomes editable in a textarea + a category dropdown appears (the [#204](https://github.com/audiocontrol-org/deskwork/issues/204) extension); Save / Cancel buttons appear.
4. Edit the text + change the category; click Save.
5. Confirm the card re-renders with the new text + new category.
6. Click Delete on a different comment; confirm via the inline-confirm dialog.
7. Confirm the comment disappears from the sidebar.
**Expected:** edit + delete affordances work; addressed-state badges render correctly.
**Verifying comment:**
```
Verified in v0.16.0 — marginalia comment cards expose Edit (text + category) and Delete affordances; edits persist; deletes tombstone via the journal. Closing.
```
**Close:** `gh issue close 199`

### [#201](https://github.com/audiocontrol-org/deskwork/issues/201) — skill-prose drift (approve / publish / iterate)

**Surface:** SKILL.md prose; verify by reading the marketplace-installed skill files.
**Walk:**
1. `cat ~/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/deskwork/plugins/deskwork/skills/approve/SKILL.md | head -20`
2. Confirm: NO `Prerequisite: sidecar.reviewState === "approved"` line; NO "click Approve in the studio first" framing. Description should reference THESIS Consequence 2 (clipboard-copy of the slash command).
3. Same check for `plugins/deskwork/skills/publish/SKILL.md` and `plugins/deskwork/skills/iterate/SKILL.md`.
**Expected:** all three skills' prerequisite gates either dropped or reframed to match the underlying CLI's actual gates.
**Verifying comment:**
```
Verified in v0.16.0 — approve/publish/iterate SKILL.md prose reflects THESIS Consequence 2 (slash-command-is-the-act); no stale reviewState gates. Closing.
```
**Close:** `gh issue close 201`

### [#202](https://github.com/audiocontrol-org/deskwork/issues/202) — split @deskwork/core/scrapbook.ts (909 → 94 + 9 modules)

**Walk:** by inspection of the installed dist.
1. `ls ~/.claude/plugins/cache/deskwork/deskwork/0.16.0/node_modules/@deskwork/core/dist/scrapbook/`
2. Expect: `crud-at-dir.js`, `crud-slug.js`, `format.js`, `listing.js`, `paths.js`, `read.js`, `seed.js`, `types.js`, `validation.js` (+ `.d.ts` siblings).
3. `wc -l ~/.claude/plugins/cache/deskwork/deskwork/0.16.0/node_modules/@deskwork/core/dist/scrapbook.js` → small barrel.
**Expected:** the barrel + 9 sibling modules in the installed dist.
**Verifying comment:**
```
Verified in v0.16.0 — @deskwork/core/scrapbook ships as a thin barrel + 9 sibling modules (types, validation, paths, listing, read, crud-at-dir, crud-slug, seed, format). Closing.
```
**Close:** `gh issue close 202`

### [#204](https://github.com/audiocontrol-org/deskwork/issues/204) — marginalia category-edit dropdown

**Surface:** `/dev/editorial-review/entry/<uuid>` → Edit a comment.
**Walk:**
1. Open a review surface with a comment.
2. Click the comment's Edit button.
3. Confirm the inline edit form contains BOTH a textarea (text edit) AND a `<select>` dropdown (category edit).
4. The dropdown's currently-selected option should match the comment's current category.
5. Change the dropdown to a different category (e.g. `voice-drift` → `clarity`).
6. Click Save.
7. Confirm the card now displays the new category in its label.
**Expected:** category-edit dropdown alongside text-edit; persists via PATCH.
**Verifying comment:**
```
Verified in v0.16.0 — comment Edit form exposes a category dropdown; selection persists; rendered card reflects new category. Closing.
```
**Close:** `gh issue close 204`

### [#205](https://github.com/audiocontrol-org/deskwork/issues/205) — scrapbook viewer + link emitters resolve via entryId

**Surface:** scrapbook viewer for a non-kebab-case entry.
**Walk:**
1. Visit `/dev/scrapbook/deskwork-internal/open-issue-tranche-cleanup/prd?entryId=b3f20364-969a-4004-87bd-278cd5992e3c`.
2. Page should render scrapbook contents from `docs/0.16.0/001-IN-PROGRESS/open-issue-tranche-cleanup/scrapbook/` (entry-aware), not from `docs/open-issue-tranche-cleanup/prd/scrapbook/` (slug-template).
3. Visit the same URL WITHOUT `?entryId=` — should resolve to the slug-template path (different scrapbook contents — likely empty).
4. Visit the dashboard at `/dev/editorial-studio` — find the PRD's scrapbook chip; hover to see the URL ends with `?entryId=…`.
5. Visit the review surface at `/dev/editorial-review/entry/b3f20364-…`; open the scrapbook drawer; the "open viewer ↗" link should also have `?entryId=`.
**Expected:** entry-aware viewer when `?entryId=` present; URLs from dashboard/drawer use the entry-aware shape.
**Verifying comment:**
```
Verified in v0.16.0 — scrapbook viewer accepts ?entryId=; URL emitters (dashboard chip, review-surface drawer) prefer entry-aware shape when an entry id is in scope. Closing.
```
**Close:** `gh issue close 205`

### [#206](https://github.com/audiocontrol-org/deskwork/issues/206) — ingest defaults to Drafting

**Surface:** CLI.
**Walk:**
1. Create a scratch markdown file with NO `state:` field in frontmatter:
   ```bash
   mkdir -p /tmp/206-verify && cat > /tmp/206-verify/test.md <<'EOF'
   ---
   title: test
   ---
   body
   EOF
   ```
2. Set up a tmp deskwork project (or reuse an existing test project).
3. Run `deskwork ingest test.md` (dry-run).
4. Read the plan's state column.
**Expected:** state column shows `Drafting` (was `Ideas` pre-fix).
**Verifying comment:**
```
Verified in v0.16.0 — deskwork ingest defaults the no-state-field case to Drafting. Closing.
```
**Close:** `gh issue close 206`

### [#207](https://github.com/audiocontrol-org/deskwork/issues/207) — split studio/src/pages/scrapbook.ts

**Walk:** by inspection of the installed dist.
1. `ls ~/.claude/plugins/cache/deskwork/deskwork-studio/0.16.0/node_modules/@deskwork/studio/dist/pages/scrapbook/`
2. Expect: `dispatch.js`, `image-readers.js`, `index.js`, `render.js`, `text-helpers.js`, `types.js` (+ `.d.ts` siblings).
**Expected:** scrapbook split landed in the dist.
**Verifying comment:**
```
Verified in v0.16.0 — @deskwork/studio ships pages/scrapbook as a thin barrel + 6 sibling modules. Closing.
```
**Close:** `gh issue close 207`

---

## Post-walk: Phase 6 tracker audit

Once all 18 walks are complete:

```bash
# 1. Snapshot remaining open issues
gh issue list --state open --limit 50 > /tmp/open-issues-post-cleanup.txt

# 2. Group manually:
#    - architecture     (#18, #30, #33 — out-of-scope per workplan)
#    - product features (#54, #84, #85, #82, #87, #86 — out-of-scope per workplan)
#    - backlog          (any new items filed during walks; #200 dual-edit-of-marginalia from operator session)
#    - external-tracking (issues with `upstream-tracking` label — should be empty after #92 closed)

# 3. Update README's status table
# 4. Confirm no "verify and close" or "moot" items remain in the open list
```

Update `docs/0.16.0/001-IN-PROGRESS/open-issue-tranche-cleanup/README.md` with the post-cleanup snapshot. Mark Phase 6 acceptance criteria as done.

## Feature complete

Once Phase 1 + Phase 6 are signed off:

```bash
/dw-lifecycle:complete open-issue-tranche-cleanup
```

This moves `docs/0.16.0/001-IN-PROGRESS/open-issue-tranche-cleanup/` → `docs/0.16.0/003-COMPLETE/open-issue-tranche-cleanup/`, updates ROADMAP if applicable, and closes any feature-tracking artifacts.

---

## Appendix — known closed-during-this-session

The following 3 issues were originally on the workplan's Phase 1 list but are already closed; no walk needed:

- **[#183](https://github.com/audiocontrol-org/deskwork/issues/183)** ingest --apply no sidecar — closed in v0.15.0
- **[#184](https://github.com/audiocontrol-org/deskwork/issues/184)** add no sidecar — closed in v0.15.0
- **[#188](https://github.com/audiocontrol-org/deskwork/issues/188)** marginalia drawer auto-open — closed in v0.15.0

And one issue was verified mid-session via the workplan iteration cycle:

- **[#190](https://github.com/audiocontrol-org/deskwork/issues/190)** marginalia vertical alignment — operator-confirmed via workplan margin note `2adcfb50` ("Confirmed — this works") on 2026-05-05.

These four are excluded from the walk.

## Appendix — closed during the sweep (no walk needed)

Phase 5 closed 8 issues during the in-session triage. These do not need verification walks:

- **[#75](https://github.com/audiocontrol-org/deskwork/issues/75)** Publish button retired in Phase 30 (THESIS-superseded)
- **[#83](https://github.com/audiocontrol-org/deskwork/issues/83)** auto-commit superseded by THESIS Consequence 2
- **[#89](https://github.com/audiocontrol-org/deskwork/issues/89)** repair-install mitigation (root cause upstream)
- **[#92](https://github.com/audiocontrol-org/deskwork/issues/92)** Claude Code platform bug (workarounds in place; operator-directed close)
- **[#94](https://github.com/audiocontrol-org/deskwork/issues/94)** Phase 26 npm-publish umbrella (shipped v0.9.5)
- **[#40](https://github.com/audiocontrol-org/deskwork/issues/40)** Phase 20 outline-in-scrapbook (shipped)
- **[#53](https://github.com/audiocontrol-org/deskwork/issues/53)** scaffoldBlogPost reframed (no production caller)
- **[#203](https://github.com/audiocontrol-org/deskwork/issues/203)** marginalia range editing (wontfix per operator scope decision)
